A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 90 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ON TYNDALL AIR FORCE BASE. THE TORNADO MOVED A CAR, BROKE CAR WINDOWS, TORE A PORTION OF A NEW ROOF OFF OF BARRACKS AND FLIPPED DUMPSTERS AND GARBAGE CANS ON THEIR SIDES.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM SURVEYED THE DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OF A DAYCARE AS WELL AS TREE DAMAGE THAT WAS FANNED OUT TO THE NORTHEAST WITH ALL TREES LYING IN THE SAME DIRECTION. AFTER THE INVESTIGATION OF THE ROOF, SPEAKING TO WITNESSES, AND THE OVERALL PATTERN OF THE TREE DAMAGE, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WAS THE CAUSE OF THE DAMAGE. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 85 TO 90 MPH.

A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR ROCK HILL ROAD AND CHANCEY MILL ROAD SOUTHWEST OF BLAKELY. A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED AND BLOWN DOWN IN DIFFERENT DIRECTIONS, AND A SMALL DUAL-POL DEBRIS SIGNATURE WAS NOTED ON KEOX RADAR. MAX WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH.
